habitat: Near rivers; ponds; lakes; seashores; freshwater wetlands; and coastal waters up to 2500 m
story: Few birds capture the imagination quite like the Pied Kingfisher (Ceryle rudis). As a member of the Alcedinidae family, it has carved out a unique niche in Near rivers; ponds; lakes; seashores; freshwater wetlands; and coastal waters up to 2500 m. Its conservation status is Least Concern (LC), a classification that drives crucial research and protection efforts. The Pied Kingfisher's presence is a vital sign of its ecosystem's health, making its preservation a priority. This poster is a tribute to the Pied Kingfisher, celebrating its role in the natural world and inspiring a deeper appreciation for biodiversity.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
